title: koeleria albovii subsp. caucasica
intro: Koeleria albovii subsp. caucasica, also known as Caucasian Koeleria, is a subspecies of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Koeleria albovii subsp. caucasica include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Koeleria albovii subsp. caucasica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
